title: Synsepalum stipulatum
taxonomy: Synsepalum stipulatum is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sapote or banana family. The Sapotaceae family is part of the larger order Ericales, which also includes other flowering plants like heath and blueberry families. The order Ericales is a group of flowering plants that are often found in tropical and temperate regions. Synsepalum stipulatum is classified within the kingdom Plantae, which includes all types of plants.

intro: Synsepalum stipulatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, commonly known as the West African miracle fruit. The health benefits of Synsepalum stipulatum include its ability to alter the taste of sour foods to sweet, making it a potential tool for managing metabolic disorders and improving dietary compliance. The bioactive constituents of this plant include a unique glycoprotein called miraculin, which is responsible for its taste-altering properties, as well as other bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Synsepalum stipulatum is often prepared as a frozen or dried fruit, and its extracts are used in various herbal preparations, including capsules, powders, and beverages, to leverage its unique health benefits.
